description: Letter from Zakkay b. Moshe to the ḍāmin (tax-farmer) Abū l-Bishr Mevasser ha-Kohen b. Salmān. The writer, a scribe in al-Maḥalla, describes his work of copying a commentary on the Torah onto vellum in big letters. He asks whether the book should be bound in that town. Dating (Goitein): ca. 1145. (Information from Mediterranean Society, I, p. 112; II, pp. 239, 605; Goitein's index cards)
